description_long: This is a tall, leafless deciduous tree with a gnarled, irregular trunk and a sparse arrangement of thin, spindly branches. The trunk is thick at the base and tapers as it rises, with several large roots visibly protruding above the ground. The branches are mostly bare, with only a few scattered, brownish leaves clinging to the tips and some fallen leaves scattered around the base. The overall silhouette is asymmetrical, with branches extending unevenly in different directions, giving the tree a weathered and aged appearance. The bark appears rough and dark, contrasting with the lighter, dry leaves. This tree would be commonly found in urban parks, along sidewalks, or in naturalized green spaces.
description: A tall, leafless deciduous tree with a thick, gnarled trunk, sparse branches, and visible roots. Scattered brown leaves cling to the branches and litter the ground, giving it a weathered, autumnal look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the thick trunk, prominent exposed roots, and sparse branches with a few scattered leaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the irregular branching pattern and the spread of roots and fallen leaves on the ground.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear silhouette of the trunk tapering upward and the sparse, spindly branches extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the uneven distribution of branches and the exposed root system.
